title: festuca subulata subsp. japonica
intro: Festuca subulata subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese wood rush, is a type of flowering plant in the grass family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating coughs and respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Festuca subulata subsp. japonica can be prepared as a tea or infusion, and its rhizomes can be dried and used in herbal preparations, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
